choice: Scenario/Forecast/Plan/Snapshot model with one active Plan
rationale: Three binding priors jointly determine this. (1) 479caa25 frames job #2 as 'compare actual trajectory vs the retirement plan' — the definite article and singular imply exactly one canonical Plan to compare against. (2) 913da4c1 establishes that the headline output is a Monte Carlo probability curve, which is the definition of a Forecast (a named, saveable simulation result); multiple such outputs can exist for comparison. (3) 5d2f9381 already introduces a Snapshot as a first-class concept ('every successful sync persists a dated balance snapshot'). Together these three prior decisions define all four nouns: Snapshot (synced balance at a date), Forecast (Monte Carlo simulation run), Plan (the single active set of retirement assumptions), and implicitly Scenario (a named bundle of inputs used to produce a Forecast). Option 2 (single implicit plan) would preclude the multi-Forecast comparison job; Option 3 (freeform saved simulations) lacks the explicit Plan/Snapshot structure already implied by 479caa25 and 5d2f9381.
